summary: 7W / 6L (53.8%) on 13 decided matchups; PIT@NYY and BAL@BOS were postponed. Of the four real-stake GOLD CONFIRMs on decided games, the slate split 2-2: SD@ATL and MIA@HOU won, SF@KC and DET@CHC lost. Two Claude DOWNGRADEs each missed 10-run blowout wins (TB@TOR on unvalidated 'flat-edge market-priced' reasoning, CHW@TEX on an n=1 prior-day overfit), both directly violating the 7/20 mandate to lean CONFIRM on GOLD unless a suppression rule has ≥70% recent track record on that exact shape. STL@LAA was suppressed by CAP7 and the edge ceiling for the second straight day despite the slate's strongest PQI confirmation.

MIN @ CLE.headline: CLE beat MIN 5-2; CONFIRM SKIP correctly withheld stake on a viability-floor-failing bottom-bucket marginal favorite.
MIN @ CLE.hypothesis: fair_prob=0.409 sat below the 0.42 viability floor independent of any other signal, CAP 10 zeroed the bottom-bucket pick (pick_prob=0.538 in (0.50,0.55]), and PQI (Δ=+17.6) opposed MIN. CLE's 3-run win validates all three suppression layers. No process failure — the fair-prob floor and CAP 10 both worked as designed.
MIN @ CLE.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob<0.42 viability floor and [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket marginal-favorite — both correctly suppressed a loser; no revision needed.

LAD @ PHI.headline: LAD edged PHI 2-1; CONFIRM SKIP zero-staked correctly even though the direction happened to be right.
LAD @ PHI.hypothesis: SP xERA (Wheeler, 1.49 for PHI), PQI (Δ=+15.7), and f5_full_delta=0.5611 (the slate's largest Stage 1/2 split) all opposed LAD, yet LAD won by 1 run in the calibration-caution edge band (23.27pp). A 1-run win against triple-opposing signals is variance, not evidence the dual-opposing rule should become a directional fade. Zero-stake was the correct sizing regardless of outcome.
LAD @ PHI.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta (0.5611) and calibration_caution_18_25pp — both correctly suppressed stake on a marginal outcome that easily could have gone the other way; dual-opposing suppression-only rule holds.

TB @ TOR.headline: TB demolished TOR 12-2 — the unvalidated 'flat-edge market-priced' DOWNGRADE to SKIP missed a 10-run blowout win.
TB @ TOR.hypothesis: Process failure. Claude downgraded on the premise that a 0.70pp edge means the market has fully priced in Gausman's advantage — an invented heuristic with no prior track record, applied in direct conflict with the 7/20 mandate to lean CONFIRM on GOLD absent a ≥70%-validated suppression rule. The underlying pre-cap stack was clean (F1*=0.86 corroborated by SP xERA+PQI+Stage; pre-cap score=5/A); TB's 10-run blowout demonstrates the flat-edge read was factually wrong, not merely unlucky.
TB @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: 'flat-edge market-priced' DOWNGRADE rationale — a novel, unvalidated suppression heuristic that missed a 10-run win; treat edge_pp near the 4pp floor as a stake-sizing boundary only, not as evidence the market has eliminated an edge, and do not apply this reasoning to override the lean-CONFIRM mandate without a prior track record.

SD @ ATL.headline: SD routed ATL 8-3 — the CAP10 CONFIRM-rescue on a PLATINUM-demoted GOLD pick paid off with a real stake.
SD @ ATL.hypothesis: Process success. Pre-cap B+ logic (F3=2777.6 well above the 1000 anchor, PQI confirms Δ=-6.9, Stage agrees) cleared the bar for rescuing a bottom-bucket favorite (pick_prob=0.538) from CAP 10's default zero. SD's 5-run win validates both the PLATINUM-demotion scrutiny and the F3>1000 anchor as a rescue qualifier. This was the day's only CAP10-rescue win; the other two rescue CONFIRMs (SF@KC, DET@CHC) lost.
SD @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] CONFIRM-rescue with F3_swing_take_gap>1000 as anchor — worked here; track cumulative rescue hit rate across all three today's real-stake applications (SD@ATL WIN, SF@KC LOSS, DET@CHC LOSS = 1-for-3) before scaling stake size.

SF @ KC.headline: KC edged SF 3-2 — a real-stake CAP10-rescue CONFIRM lost by 1 run.
SF @ KC.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate, though the 1-run margin keeps variance in play. The rescue cited four-dimension corroboration (SP xERA +0.65 agrees, PQI Δ=-14.5 confirms, Stage agrees, independent of starred F1_xera_gap=1.01*) but F3=822.2 sat below the 1000-anchor threshold — a weaker profile than the day's only rescue win (SD@ATL, F3=2777.6). Combined with DET@CHC's 9-run loss on nearly identical rescue justification, the CAP10-rescue rule went 1-for-3 on real stakes today; an F3>1000 minimum may be needed specifically for bottom-bucket rescues.
SF @ KC.signals_to_recheck: four-dimension CAP-10 rescue — 1-for-3 on today's real stakes; F3<1000 corroboration (822.2 here) may be a weaker rescue qualifier than F3>1000; consider requiring F3>1000 as a minimum before staking a CAP10 rescue rather than accepting any SP+PQI+Stage independent combination.

NYM @ MIL.headline: NYM shut out MIL 4-0 — the sub-1000-F3 DOWNGRADE correctly protected against a real loss.
NYM @ MIL.hypothesis: Process success. F3=243.9 was far below the 1000 meaningful-anchor threshold, PQI opposed MIL (Δ=-9.0), acute_roster and confidence_downgrade both fired, and f5_full_delta=0.1911 reflected Stage 1/2 divergence. MIL being shut out validates the DOWNGRADE cleanly — a genuinely thin B- stack, not a caps-suppressed strong pick, exactly as the reasoning distinguished. This is the correct use of the sub-1000-F3 rule.
NYM @ MIL.signals_to_recheck: sub-1000 F3_swing_take_gap as a non-anchor — correctly flagged a losing GOLD pick; archive continues to validate F3<1000 alone as insufficient for a GOLD-tier stake.

DET @ CHC.headline: CHC blew out DET 11-2 — the strongest four-dimension CAP10-rescue CONFIRM of the slate lost by 9 runs.
DET @ CHC.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. The reasoning cited a genuine pre-cap A stack (F1=1.82 not starred, F3=1761.1 above 1000, SP edge 1.13 agrees, PQI confirms Δ=-13.2, Stage agrees) — on paper the cleanest of today's three CAP10-rescue CONFIRMs. The 9-run loss against that profile points toward an unforeseeable in-game gap (Valdez / CHC offense outperforming projection) rather than a flawed rescue rule, but the magnitude is large enough that whether it is process or variance failure is genuinely uncertain. Log alongside SF@KC as a second same-day loss on identical rescue justification.
DET @ CHC.signals_to_recheck: four-dimension CAP-10 rescue — second same-day real-stake loss despite F3>1000 and all four dimensions affirming; check hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap and pen_strain_pick_side retroactively to determine whether CHC's offensive blowout was foreseeable from pre-game data.

CHW @ TEX.headline: TEX shut out CHW 10-0 in the identical Rocker-vs-Schultz matchup — the n=1 prior-day-loss rule missed a 10-run win.
CHW @ TEX.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate. The 7/20 real-money OVERRIDE of CAP 7 in this exact matchup lost 10-3 — a legitimate lesson about not overriding CAP 7 via starter reputation. But that single loss was hardened into a blanket 'archive rule' that DOWNGRADEd today's otherwise-strong GOLD pick (F1*=2.04, SP edge 1.31 agrees, PQI confirms +14.9, Stage agree) entirely to SKIP. TEX won by 10 today. The 7/20 lesson addressed the override mechanism, not the directional pick; converting a single override loss into a same-direction DOWNGRADE on the very next occurrence violated the ≥70% rule-validation mandate.
CHW @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage sub-0.02 band — a 10-run win missed the day after a 10-run loss because of overcorrection from OVERRIDE to DOWNGRADE; the sub-0.02 band pattern remains net-positive in the archive, suggesting half-Kelly sizing is preferable to alternating between full-stake and zero-stake game to game.

MIA @ HOU.headline: HOU beat MIA 5-3 — a real-stake CONFIRM on an already-penalized B-/score=1 stack came through.
MIA @ HOU.hypothesis: Process success. F3=1270 and F5=+1.14 provided multi-signal support with pick_prob=0.5991 outside the bottom bucket (no CAP 10 rescue required), and the model had already sized this down from PLATINUM to reflect PQI's mild opposition (Δ=-8.3). HOU's win validates both the underlying signals and the judgment that the BAL-series caution from 7/17-7/19 did not transfer to a structurally different MIA opponent.
MIA @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: None — F3/F5 multi-signal GOLD with pick_prob outside the bottom bucket converted cleanly; no revision needed.

WSH @ COL.headline: COL walked off WSH 8-7 — the sub-4pp edge floor enforced SKIP, correctly avoiding a real stake on a pick that went the wrong way.
WSH @ COL.hypothesis: Process success on sizing, direction wrong. The binding constraint was edge=2.53pp below the 4pp floor — a code-level stake-sizing gate, not a signal failure. WSH lost in a 1-run walkoff despite a pre-cap A stack (F2+F5 fire, SP xERA agrees, PQI confirms Δ=-6.3). Yesterday (7/20) the identical matchup saw WSH win 7-3 while the same floor blocked the stake. The floor has now been wrong once (7/20) and right once (7/21) in the same matchup on consecutive days — pure noise at this edge magnitude.
WSH @ COL.signals_to_recheck: edge-floor-4pp binding — now 1-1 on WSH@COL consecutive days (missed WIN on 7/20, avoided LOSS on 7/21); treat sub-4pp edge as a pure stake-sizing variance zone with no directional information.

STL @ LAA.headline: LAA beat STL 5-1 — CAP7 and the 15pp edge ceiling suppressed a real stake on the slate's strongest PQI confirmation for the second straight day.
STL @ LAA.hypothesis: Process-failure candidate on sizing, not direction. F1=2.49 not starred, SP edge 1.97 (exceptional), PQI +34.2 (slate's strongest confirmation) — directionally perfect and LAA won by 4. The tier was zeroed by CAP 7 (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0080, sub-0.02 band) and the 15pp edge ceiling (17.77pp>15pp), both code-level gates rather than signal doubt. This is the second consecutive day (7/20: LAA won 3-2; 7/21: LAA won 5-1) that these gates zeroed a real stake on the model's strongest-PQI call.
STL @ LAA.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] sub-0.02 band and the 15pp edge ceiling — now suppressed STL@LAA stakes on two consecutive days (7/20 LAA won 3-2, 7/21 LAA won 5-1) with the slate's strongest PQI confirmation both times; PQI>+30 combined with SP-edge>1.5 should be evaluated as a potential partial-Kelly carve-out from the edge ceiling rather than a hard zero-stake.

OAK @ ARI.headline: ARI edged OAK 6-5 — the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO correctly withheld stake; ARI winning is incidental to the veto's purpose.
OAK @ ARI.hypothesis: Kohl Drake's 0-Statcast-pitch current-season sample triggered a non-negotiable HARD_VETO independent of direction. ARI winning 6-5 in a 1-run game is a favorable but incidental outcome — the veto functions as a data-availability gate, not a directional prediction, and the closeness of the result shows genuine uncertainty that the veto correctly reflected.
OAK @ ARI.signals_to_recheck: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO on thin_sp_data — continues to function correctly as a non-directional data-availability gate; no revision needed.

CIN @ SEA.headline: CIN beat SEA 4-2 — the dual-opposing D-grade SKIP correctly avoided a real loss.
CIN @ SEA.hypothesis: Process success. SP xERA (Castillo, 1.39 edge for CIN) and PQI (Δ=-19.6) both opposed SEA, and CIN won validating the read. Per the settled archive rule this remains suppression-only (zero stake on SEA, no directional fade toward CIN), but the opposing direction called the correct side again — D-grade dual-opposing continues to be directionally accurate more often than not.
CIN @ SEA.signals_to_recheck: SP_xERA/PQI dual-opposing at grade D — correctly predicted the loser again; consistent with suppression-only treatment, not a promotion to a directional fade.

PIT @ NYY.headline: Game postponed — no result to grade.
PIT @ NYY.hypothesis: PIT @ NYY did not complete on 2026-07-21. No outcome to analyze. The DOWNGRADE reasoning (F5-only GOLD anchor insufficient, SP xERA and PQI both opposing NYY, grade D/score=-1, bullpen_fatigue) stands on its merits but cannot be validated without a result.

BAL @ BOS.headline: Game postponed — no result to grade.
BAL @ BOS.hypothesis: BAL @ BOS did not complete on 2026-07-21. No outcome to analyze. The CONFIRM reasoning (pre-cap A/score=5: F1*=0.76 corroborated by SP xERA+PQI+Stage four-dimension affirm, negative bullpen gap, edge 8.9pp in valid window) was the strongest unconditional GOLD CONFIRM on the slate and cannot be validated without a result.

patterns_observed: Two DOWNGRADEs today (TB@TOR 'flat-edge market-priced' reasoning, CHW@TEX 'same-matchup-prior-day-loss' blanket rule) each missed a 10-run blowout win, directly violating the 7/20 mandate to lean CONFIRM on GOLD unless a suppression rule has ≥70% recent track record on that exact shape. Treating a sub-1pp edge as 'market has priced it in' and converting a single prior-day override loss into a standing DOWNGRADE rule are both n=1 generalizations with no validation history — together they cost two missed 10-run wins on the same slate.

The three CAP10-rescue CONFIRMs (SD@ATL F3=2777.6 WIN, SF@KC F3=822.2 LOSS, DET@CHC F3=1761.1 LOSS) went 1-for-3 on real stakes. F3>1000 did not cleanly separate winners from losers (DET@CHC had it and lost by 9 runs), suggesting the rescue rule's success depends on a factor not yet isolated. Track cumulative CAP10-rescue real-stake hit rate separately and hold stake size at minimum GOLD until at least 10 rescue instances accumulate.

STL@LAA has been suppressed by [HARD CAP 7] sub-0.02 band plus the 15pp edge ceiling on two consecutive days (7/20 LAA won 3-2, 7/21 LAA won 5-1), each time with the slate's strongest PQI confirmation (Δ>+30) and SP edge>1.5. Two same-shape suppressed wins is sufficient to evaluate whether PQI>+30 combined with SP-edge>1.5 should earn a partial-Kelly carve-out from the edge ceiling rather than a hard zero-stake.

WSH@COL's sub-4pp edge floor produced opposite outcomes on consecutive days in the identical matchup (missed WIN on 7/20, correctly avoided LOSS on 7/21), confirming the sub-4pp zone is pure stake-sizing noise with no directional information. Stop citing WSH@COL prior-day results as input for CONFIRM or DOWNGRADE logic; treat each sub-4pp-edge game as fully independent of the preceding day's outcome.
